mnemonics: CLT = Central Limit Theorem, CLT = Confidence Limits Too
keyPoints: The Central Limit Theorem states that the distribution of sample means approaches a normal distribution as the sample size increases.
The Confidence Limit for unknown mean is calculated using the formula: x̄ ± (Z * (σ / √n))
where x̄ is the sample mean, Z is the Z-score, σ is the population standard deviation, and n is the sample size.
